refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a key performance indicator (KPI) requirement that directly impacts customer productivity and system acceptance. Refinement adds explicit reference to measurement/monitoring (telemetry) and duty cycle context to make verification concrete. High safety criticality tailoring requires that cycle time optimization does not compromise safety interlocks or emergency response.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Instrumented field trials under representative duty cycle; p95 cycle time computed from telemetry logs; comparison against CT_target acceptance threshold; safety event correlation analysis to ensure no safety compromise.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-002.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within design envelope.
FR-NGHE-002.2: The NGHE solution shall collect and transmit cycle time telemetry (start, end, sub-phase timestamps) to enable p95 KPI calculation.
FR-NGHE-002.3: The NGHE solution shall not sacrifice safety-critical response times (e.g., emergency stop, stability checks) in pursuit of cycle time targets.
FR-NGHE-002.4: The teleoperation subsystem (if engaged) shall not introduce latency that degrades p95 cycle time beyond acceptable margin.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Optimize vehicle powertrain (engine, transmission, hydraulics) for peak efficiency and responsiveness; instrument with real-time cycle phase sensors; implement predictive load balancing.
DP-B: Implement centralized cycle orchestration logic that sequences load→travel→dump phases with minimal inter-phase delays; use deterministic scheduling and priority-based task management.
DP-C: Deploy telemetry collection and KPI computation at the edge (vehicle) and cloud (backend); establish feedback loop to identify and eliminate cycle time bottlenecks.
DP-D: Integrate safety-aware cycle optimization that enforces hard constraints on emergency response time and stability margins; use formal verification for safety-critical paths.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-B + DP-C + DP-D (integrated): Implement a deterministic cycle orchestration engine (DP-B) that sequences load→travel→dump phases with minimal inter-phase delays, coupled with edge+cloud telemetry collection (DP-C) to measure and report p95 cycle time, and safety-aware constraint enforcement (DP-D) to ensure emergency response and stability margins are never compromised. Vehicle powertrain optimization (DP-A) is a supporting design activity but not the primary DP for this FR.

potential_sources: Cycle orchestration logic (DP-B) must interface cleanly with vehicle control subsystems (powertrain, hydraulics, steering) without creating tight coupling.
Telemetry collection (DP-C) must not introduce overhead that degrades cycle time performance.
Safety constraint enforcement (DP-D) must be decoupled from performance optimization to avoid conflicts.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP is a composition of three sub-DPs that address different aspects of cycle time KPI: orchestration logic, measurement/feedback, and safety guardrails. Decoupling is achieved via well-defined interfaces (Tasking for orchestration commands, Telemetry for KPI data, SafetyAlert for constraint violations). No circular dependencies detected.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Vehicle powertrain is capable of meeting cycle time targets under nominal conditions; risk: if powertrain is undersized, no orchestration logic can compensate.

Assumption: Telemetry infrastructure (sensors, network) has sufficient bandwidth and latency to support real-time cycle phase tracking; risk: network congestion or sensor failures could degrade KPI visibility.

Assumption: Safety constraints (emergency stop, stability checks) can be formalized and enforced without introducing unacceptable latency; risk: overly conservative safety margins could make CT_target unachievable.

Assumption: Duty cycle definition is stable and representative of actual site conditions; risk: field variability (soil, load mass, operator skill) could cause p95 to exceed target despite lab validation.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-B to explicitly specify the cycle orchestration state machine (load phase entry/exit conditions, travel phase routing, dump phase completion criteria) and define the interface contract with vehicle control subsystems. Refine DP-C to specify telemetry sampling rate, edge vs. cloud computation split, and KPI aggregation window (e.g., rolling 100-cycle p95). Refine DP-D to document the formal safety constraints and verification method (e.g., model checking, simulation).

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: The Vehicle module owns the cycle orchestration engine (DP-B), real-time cycle phase sensors and actuators, and edge-side telemetry collection (DP-C). The Vehicle module also enforces safety-aware cycle constraints (DP-D) via integration with Safety Services. The Vehicle module is responsible for executing the load→travel→dump cycle within the CT_target envelope and reporting cycle phase timestamps via Telemetry interface.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives cycle task commands (load location, travel destination, dump site) from NGHE Solution orchestrator; triggers cycle orchestration state machine.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Transmits cycle phase timestamps (load start/end, travel start/end, dump start/end), cycle duration, and p95 KPI metrics to backend for aggregation and reporting.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives safety constraint updates from Safety Services (e.g., emergency stop, stability margin adjustments); transmits safety-critical events (e.g., constraint violation, emergency stop trigger) back to Safety Services.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Provides connectivity for Telemetry and SafetyAlert transmission; may also support Teleoperation latency-sensitive commands if tele-operated mode is active.

module_containment_scores.notes: Cycle orchestration, phase sensors, and edge telemetry are naturally contained within Vehicle. Safety constraint enforcement is co-owned with Safety Services via SafetyAlert interface (minimal diffusion). Recommended primary owner.

NGHE Solution could host the cycle orchestration logic at the system level, but this would require Vehicle to be a passive executor, creating tight coupling and diffusing cycle timing logic across system and vehicle layers. Not recommended as primary owner.

Teleoperation is responsible for remote operator commands and latency management, not cycle orchestration or KPI measurement. Containment score is low; diffusion would be high if cycle logic were placed here.

Safety Services owns safety constraint enforcement (DP-D), but not cycle orchestration or telemetry collection. Shared responsibility via SafetyAlert interface is appropriate; full containment would diffuse cycle timing logic into safety domain.

assigned_im.ownership_candidate: Vehicle (primary owner for DP-B and DP-C; co-owner with Safety Services for DP-D via SafetyAlert interface).
assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Cycle orchestration logic, phase sensors, and edge telemetry collection are functionally and physically cohesive and should be contained within the Vehicle module. Safety constraint enforcement is a cross-cutting concern that is best managed via a well-defined SafetyAlert interface between Vehicle and Safety Services, avoiding diffusion of safety logic into the vehicle control layer.
containment_risks: Risk: If cycle orchestration logic is not cleanly separated from low-level vehicle control (powertrain, hydraulics), changes to cycle timing could inadvertently affect safety-critical behavior. Mitigation: Define a clear orchestration API that Vehicle control subsystems must implement; use formal verification for safety-critical paths.

Risk: Telemetry collection overhead (sensor polling, data buffering, transmission) could introduce latency that degrades cycle time performance. Mitigation: Profile telemetry overhead; use asynchronous, non-blocking collection; prioritize critical phase timestamps.

Risk: Safety Services may impose conservative constraints (e.g., reduced acceleration during load phase) that make CT_target unachievable. Mitigation: Collaborate with Safety Manager to define safety margins that are tight but achievable; use simulation to validate feasibility before field trials.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Explicitly define the Vehicle module's internal architecture: (1) Cycle Orchestration Engine (state machine, phase transition logic), (2) Phase Sensor Integration (load cell, position sensors, timing), (3) Edge Telemetry Collector (buffering, aggregation, transmission), (4) Safety Constraint Enforcer (integration point with SafetyAlert). Document the API contract between Cycle Orchestration Engine and vehicle control subsystems (powertrain, hydraulics, steering). Specify telemetry sampling rates and edge vs. cloud computation split for KPI calculation.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (cycle orchestration + telemetry + safety constraints) directly addresses the FR requirement to achieve p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target. The DP is decomposable into sub-DPs (DP-B, DP-C, DP-D) that are individually verifiable and collectively sufficient. No independence violations detected; the three sub-DPs address orthogonal aspects (orchestration, measurement, safety).

diffusion_risks: Cycle orchestration logic (DP-B) is contained within Vehicle, but must interface cleanly with NGHE Solution (Tasking) and Safety Services (SafetyAlert) to avoid diffusion. Mitigation: Define formal interface contracts.

Telemetry collection (DP-C) spans Vehicle (edge collection) and backend (cloud aggregation). Diffusion is acceptable here as it reflects the distributed nature of KPI computation. Mitigation: Clearly separate edge vs. cloud responsibilities.

Safety constraint enforcement (DP-D) is co-owned by Vehicle and Safety Services. Diffusion is minimized via SafetyAlert interface. Mitigation: Ensure SafetyAlert is the sole communication channel for safety constraints.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module achieves 0.95 containment score for the integrated DP. The 0.05 diffusion is acceptable and reflects the necessary co-ownership of safety constraints with Safety Services. No alternative module achieves better containment without introducing unacceptable coupling or diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible via instrumented field trials: (1) Deploy cycle phase sensors and telemetry collection on Vehicle; (2) Execute representative duty cycle under site conditions; (3) Compute p95 cycle time from telemetry logs; (4) Compare against CT_target. Safety verification requires formal analysis of constraint enforcement logic and simulation of edge cases (e.g., emergency stop during load phase).
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: Vehicle owns cycle orchestration, phase sensing, and edge telemetry. NGHE Solution owns task scheduling and system-level KPI reporting. Safety Services owns constraint enforcement and safety event handling. Interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and traceable to FR sub-requirements.

recommended_revisions: Refine FR-NGHE-002.3 to explicitly define the safety-critical response times (e.g., emergency stop latency ≤ 100ms) that must not be compromised by cycle time optimization.
Refine DP-B to specify the cycle orchestration state machine in formal notation (e.g., UML state diagram, Timed Automata) to enable verification.
Refine DP-C to specify the telemetry sampling rate (e.g., 10 Hz for phase timestamps, 1 Hz for aggregate metrics) and edge vs. cloud computation split.
Refine DP-D to document the formal safety constraints (e.g., acceleration limits, stability margins) and verification method (e.g., model checking, simulation).
Add a traceability matrix linking FR-NGHE-002 and sub-FRs to DP-B, DP-C, DP-D and Vehicle module responsibilities.

decision.justification: The refined FR-NGHE-002 and selected DP are well-formed and pass all gate reviews. However, the DP is a composition of three sub-DPs (orchestration, telemetry, safety constraints) that require further decomposition to be implementable. The Vehicle module assignment is sound, but the internal architecture of the Vehicle module (Cycle Orchestration Engine, Phase Sensor Integration, Edge Telemetry Collector, Safety Constraint Enforcer) must be decomposed into lower-level FRs and DPs. Additionally, the sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-002.1 through FR-NGHE-002.4) require individual decomposition to guide detailed design and implementation.
decision.justification_detail: This is a strategic decomposition level that establishes the system-level approach to cycle time KPI achievement. The next level must decompose the Vehicle module's internal architecture and detail the cycle orchestration state machine, telemetry collection strategy, and safety constraint enforcement mechanism. This is appropriate for a ZIGZAG_DOWN decision.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-002.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within design envelope.
FR-NGHE-002.2: The NGHE solution shall collect and transmit cycle time telemetry (start, end, sub-phase timestamps) to enable p95 KPI calculation.
FR-NGHE-002.3: The NGHE solution shall not sacrifice safety-critical response times (e.g., emergency stop, stability checks) in pursuit of cycle time targets.
FR-NGHE-002.4: The teleoperation subsystem (if engaged) shall not introduce latency that degrades p95 cycle time beyond acceptable margin.

assumptions: Vehicle powertrain is capable of meeting cycle time targets under nominal conditions.
Telemetry infrastructure (sensors, network) has sufficient bandwidth and latency to support real-time cycle phase tracking.
Safety constraints (emergency stop, stability checks) can be formalized and enforced without introducing unacceptable latency.
Duty cycle definition is stable and representative of actual site conditions.
CT_target is achievable without compromising safety margins or operator comfort.
Cycle orchestration logic can be decoupled from low-level vehicle control via a well-defined API.
open_risks: Powertrain undersizing: If vehicle powertrain is undersized, no orchestration logic can compensate. Mitigation: Validate powertrain capability early in design phase.
Telemetry overhead: Telemetry collection could introduce latency that degrades cycle time. Mitigation: Profile overhead; use asynchronous collection.
Safety margin conflicts: Safety constraints may be too conservative to achieve CT_target. Mitigation: Collaborate with Safety Manager to define tight but achievable margins.
Field variability: Actual site conditions may differ from duty cycle assumptions. Mitigation: Conduct field trials under representative conditions; implement adaptive cycle optimization.

Teleoperation latency: If tele-operated mode is active, network latency could degrade p95 cycle time. Mitigation: Define acceptable latency budget; implement predictive control for tele-operated cycles.
evidence_needed: Powertrain performance data (acceleration, speed, torque curves) under representative load conditions.
Telemetry infrastructure specifications (sensor types, sampling rates, network bandwidth, latency).
Safety constraint definitions (emergency stop latency, stability margins, acceleration limits) from Safety Manager.
Duty cycle specification (load mass, travel distance, terrain, operator skill level) from Site Manager.
CT_target value and acceptance criteria from customer.
Teleoperation latency budget and acceptable degradation margin from Tele-operator.
Formal verification results for cycle orchestration state machine and safety constraint enforcement logic.
Field trial data (p95 cycle time, safety events, telemetry logs) from representative site conditions.
